articles.title: Dinosaur Footprints Found on a Beach!
articles.summary: Scientists found dinosaur footprints on a beach in South Africa. The tracks are 132 million years old, making them the youngest dinosaur tracks ever found in southern Africa. A team was looking for a dinosaur tooth when they spotted the footprints. The area is small, only as long as a school bus, but it had dozens of tracks. This shows dinosaurs lived there long after massive volcanic eruptions. The footprints are covered by the ocean twice a day.

articles.title: Ancient Snake Had Legs and a Lost Cheekbone!
articles.summary: Scientists studied a fossil snake from Argentina that lived 100 million years ago. This snake, called Najash rionegrina, had hind legs and a cheekbone that modern snakes don't have. The fossil helps explain how snakes evolved from lizards. It shows that early snakes were big-bodied and big-mouthed, not small burrowers as scientists once thought. The team used special CT scans to see inside the fossil without breaking it. This discovery corrects a 160-year-old mistake about snake skulls.

articles.title: Huge Lava Tube Discovered on Venus!
articles.summary: Scientists found a giant underground lava tube on Venus by looking at old radar images from NASA's Magellan probe. The tube is at least 375 meters deep and could be up to 1 kilometer wide—bigger than any lava tube on Earth or Mars. It was spotted near a volcano called Nyx Mons. The discovery will help future missions like NASA's VERITAS and Europe's EnVision, which will study Venus with better radar. Venus is very hot and has crushing pressure, so humans can't visit this cave.
